headline: S&P Global Stock Has Been a Rollercoaster. Hold on Tight.
summary: By Feb. 11, the stock pick had tumbled 26% post-publication to $390, leaving Barron’s readers and investors puzzled and frustrated: “Been punished right out of [the] gate,” one commenter wrote.  For those Barron’s readers who invested in S&P Global before the post-publication selloff, it is a personal decision—as all investment decisions are—whether to cut their losses and redeploy remaining capital elsewhere.  S&P Global’s stock has since rebounded 9.3% since its February sell-off lows—ironically outpacing the 5% increase of its namesake benchmark the S&P 500 over the same period.
